clean_review_text: This is a pretty basic toothbrush. It lacks the features found on more expensive toothbrushes, such as sculpted bristle patterns, but it performs just fine. The bristles are a medium/soft density which works well for me -- firm enough for good cleaning but soft enough that it doesn't cause any gum irritation. The handles are also pretty basic but the bamboo makes them more grippy than the basic plastic brushes. The bristles are all firmly attached. None have come loose after a good amount of use. I can't say the charcoal infused bristles do anything beyond what a regular bristle does, but they look nice. Each comes individually packed and includes a tiny tube of toothpaste. It's a great item to have on hand for situations such as house guests who forget to bring a brush, for traveling, to stow in your desk at work, etc. No complaints. They work well, and I'm glad that it's a way to use/discard less plastic waste.

product_title: Pure Hyaluronic Acid Serum with Vitamin C for Face - Organic Anti Aging & Anti-Wrinkle Skin Treatment - Penetrates Deeply & Activates Collagen Production for a Younger, Firmer & Brighter Complexion
clean_review_text: Hyaluronic Acid? I never knew what it was until recently :) sounds like something you use in the brakes on your car. hyaluronic acid (also known as hyaluronan) is a glycosaminoglycan, a type of molecule composed partly of sugars. Hyaluronic acid is actually a natural structural component of skin, and, in fact, is present in connective tissue throughout the human body. The awesome thing about Hydrolic Acid is its ability to retain moisture; it’s considered to have a greater capacity to hold moisture than any other natural or synthetic polymer. In fact, one gram of hyaluronic acid is able to hold up to six liters of water. The hallmarks of youthful skin is its moisture content. A dried up face will age you quicker then anything. Hyaluronic acid can improve your skin’s moisture content and at the same time strengthen skin’s barrier; that is, the outer layers of your skin. A healthy barrier looks and feels softer, smoother, and plumper. This does all of that. Sense menopause I have suffered from dry skin. This has made a big difference in how my face looks and feels. Thank you Derma-nu for another 5 star product and Thank you for letting me try this at a reduced rate for my hoest review

product_title: SOGUE Sonic Electric Toothbrush Holders with 5 Replacement Heads Made of Dupont Bristles, 45 Days of Electricity 39,000 Vibration Motor Mouthwashes Tool for Adults Children(s71-5 Black)
clean_review_text: 10-year-old super mega-brand vs. SOGUE: 1. The SOGUE operates 10K VBM (vibrations/minute) faster. That's why I choose the SOGUE. 2. Generic replacement brushes aren't interchangeable between the two. The SOGUE has a smaller shaft. The brushes are also smaller. 3. The SOGUE comes with like a year's supply of brushes, unlike the super mega-brand. They are just a tad softer than I prefer. It took me some years to technically discriminate my preferred bristle adjective (soft, medium, hard) for re-order. My brush changing rule is 'new brush for every recharge' as brushes are now so inexpensive. The charges on these toothbrushes have got to be the longest-lasting rechargeable device in the house. It seems like months go by on a charge. 4. The SOGUE and brush are about a quarter smaller. It's a noticeable difference. After a minute of thought, I think smaller is likely better as your teeth and the spaces in between are much smaller than you think. Maybe it's getting in tighter corners. 5. The SOGUE charges in like 2 hours vs. overnight for the super mega-brand. I was looking at the super mega-brand's newest models at the big-box store. The price is absurd given all the competition from suppliers like SOGUE. The latest super mega-brand models appear the same size as the SOGUE. At a third of the price and assuming the charge life is long, it's a no-brainer. I'll adapt. Most of the difference between the two is a matter of personal preference. SOGUE wins for now. Better than advertised. Was my review helpful? If so, click "Helpful" so I can know.

clean_review_title: Handy headwear for chilly days, but snug and definitely not for windy days or extreme cold.
clean_review_text: I've only had this a few days but used it quite a bit (we're having a cold snap!) and it's handy and versatile. I like that the neck part is long, so you can either bunch it up like in the picture or tuck the bottom under the collar of your coat and zip your coat up to keep your neck extra cozy. I was hoping the neck piece could be slipped over my nose easily, but with the hood up if just doesn't work well. You CAN, but if the hood is up it makes it bulge out or fall off unless you scrunch up the draw chords (which I think makes your face look like it's poking out out a sphincter). I like that I can flip the hood back and just use the neck part, then just slip the hood back on if my ears get cold - saves me having to stop what I'm doing and look for a hat. It's of medium quality construction (fine for the price) and very useful for the right weather conditions. It's warm unless there's wind, which seems to cut through it pretty easy and chill my ears. But that also lets the air move so my head doesn't sweat or get over-hot, which is nice. Definitely not the thing for super cold (below 20F) weather or if there's a chilly wind. The faux fur is pretty soft and feels nice, and lines the entire hood and neck (not just around the face like many hoods) which I like.... even though it's not enough to keep the wind out, it does feel nice to wear. It's snug on me and I have a small head for an adult. It'd be cuter if the hood were a little looser. Would be cozy on a kid - definitely not for anyone with a big head. Overall a fine buy for the money, but it's something I'll use while working outside on cold but not very windy days - I'll definitely choose something else for around town.

clean_review_text: I give the Utopia Stainless Steel 12oz Frothing Pitcher 5 stars. I will be buying more of these pitchers because they are great for more than just being used to froth milk. I am showing pictures of my red Keurig and this 12oz pitcher is used to catch the coffee. The mugs we use for coffee are typically too large to use with our Mini Keurig, except the small mugs that came with our set of dishes. My son has a 20oz travel mug & my other son and I like to use our 16oz reusable Starbucks mugs with lids. We had been using our 8oz Pyrex measuring cup to catch the coffee, but I hate the way it dribbles liquid when you pour. We tried a few other things but they didn't fit or didn't pour without dribbles. I searched the reviews for all the 12oz frothing pitchers, and I want to say "Thank You" to everyone that reviews honestly, it really helps me to make an informed decision. This really sounded like the one I needed and was the only one that was mentioned for pouring without dribbling. The frothing pitcher was delivered in a small Utopia box. When I opened the box it was in perfect condition, no rust or dings. I did notice the word Utopia was on the pitcher and a few people mentioned that in the reviews, but it is etched so lightly I can barely see it. I even took pictures of the side with the company logo so you could see how it is barely visible. Using this with our Mini Keurig is wonderful! It pours just as other reviewers stated, it does NOT dribble. It cleans up quick. It looks nice and shiny after several weeks of being used and washed several times a day. I will be purchasing more. I want one just dedicated to frothing milk, one to use as a creamer and a few just to have. Other reviewers mentioned using it to serve warm syrup or gravy.

product_title: Haier HLP21N Portable Top Load Washer with Stainless Steel Tub
clean_review_text: I downsized and moved from a house into a 500-square foot studio apartment in the city back in February. One of the sacrifices I had to make -- or so I thought -- was private indoor laundry at home. This little Haier changed that for me. It is the smartest $200 or so that I have spent in a long time. It is so cute and small, it fits right into my walk-in closet next to the bathroom. Works like a charm, I just move the washer out of the closet and a couple of feet into the bathroom, snap the hose onto the bathroom faucet and put the drain hose into the tub. I can do a whole week's worth of laundry in a couple of hours. It holds roughly 1/3 to 1/2 of what most people would consider a normal load of clothes. In one wash load, I can do maybe 4 or 5 pairs of pants, or a full set of queen sheets (fitted bottom and flat top sheet); two large bath towels and two face towels.... you get the picture. It is too small to wash my comfortor so I just do that once a month in the building laundromat downstairs. I figure this machine has saved me at least $5/week in washing machine costs; probably more than that. So at this point the machine has already paid for itself. I've never had any leaking problems except once when I experimented and washed a blanket that was too large for the machine. There was a small puddle on the bathroom floor afterwards; no big deal. As long as you don't overstuff it, you shouldn't have any leaking issues. It's also important to me that this machine is nice and new and CLEAN... I don't have to wonder who used the machine before me or what they put in it. On a lighter note, this washing machine has also turned out to be something of a conversation piece. Whenever I have guests over and they see the washer next to the bathroom, they always comment on how cute and convenient it is. I'm debating whether to get the Haier portable dryer; for now I use my shower curtain rod as a clothes line or I just do a bunch of loads of laundry, and take them downstairs to the laundromat dryer in one shot. But it is so nice to have the option of doing all my laundry entirely in my apartment if I choose. Huge money saver and convenience. I highly recommend this machine. The only (small) complaint I have about it is not really a complaint but more of a question. The drain hose and water line are very short. I wish they were a little longer so that I didn't have to move the washer into the bathroom (three feet away) to hook it up to the faucet. I'm thinking of going to Home Depot and seeing if I can finding extensions for the water hose and the drain hose that would be long enough to reach into the bathroom. If anyone has had any luck with that, please let me know.

product_title: Whirlpool : GU2475XTVY 24 Fully Integrated Dishwasher 6 Automatic Cycles Stainless Steel
clean_review_title: Impressive performance at a good price
clean_review_text: As usual, I spent a whole lot of time looking for a replacement dishwasher. Looked at a lot of models, and narrowed the choices down to models from Whirlpool, Bosch and KitchenAid. The high end Bosch and Kitchenaid models were great - read nothing but good things (for the most part) about their performance, and they are very, very quiet. But, cost was a factor, and I just couldn't see paying more than one grand for a dishwasher (and some are a LOT more than a grand!). Looked into Whirlpool since they get excellent reliability ratings from the "consumer" magazine. This model caught my eye since it offered a fully-integrated panel (i.e., the controls are hidden) and some nice convenience features. So far, it has been a top-notch performer. The potscrubber feature works very well, and is identical to the one on the Kitchenaid (not unexpected, since Whirlpool owns Kitchenaid). Love the movable cutlery basket! You can hang it on the door for more room, or put it on the front or side of the lower tray. If loaded properly, silverware comes out clean no matter where you put the basket. If, however, you tend to just toss in forks and spoons and not arrange them, you'll want to put the basket on the lower rack so it gets more direct spray time. The interior is light gray with a tile-type pattern. It's plastic, so it's not as fancy looking as stainless. But, I care more about what the dishwasher looks like from the outside, and the stainless steel outside looks great. It also has a unique feature among hidden control models in that the steam vent is on the front of the machine, and not the top. That's important to keep the steam from hitting the bottom of your counters. Can't figure out why everyone else doesn't do it that way. Many different cycles to choose from including regular, adaptive (which uses sensors to figure out what to do), high temp and sanitary rinse. Lots of nice little touches, such as foldable tines and clips to hold light plastic items so they don't flip over during the wash cycle. Noise levels are very low. No, it is not as whisper quiet as a higher end machine, but the sound level is not at all objectionable. Excellent value - lots of features, works well, looks great, holds a boat-load of dishes, and has lots of convenience features. Can't ask for more than that! >>>>>>>Update June 2010: It's been a year since I've had this, and I'm still 100% satisfied with the machine. I run about 2-3 loads per week and so far I've not had one item not come out perfectly clean. I use an enzyme-based detergent and the rinse-aid. Settings that work for me are adaptive wash with the high-temp option. I usually run the machine after dinner and then unload it the next morning. To save on electricity, I don't use the heated dry. I find that dishes come out completely dry, but plastic items are sometimes damp, especially if it's humid out. Usually the plastic is not damp enough to warrant a towel dry except for a few pieces that I have with a recessed base - enough water collects in the indentation to warrant a quick pass with a towel before putting it back into the shelf. I've tried the sanitizing rinse and that does help dry items a little bit better. I tend to use that only when I have really greasy dishes and want an extra high temp rinse to ensure all grease is gone. Zero mechanical problems so far. Hope this keeps up! >>>>Update March 2012: Working as good as the day I bought it. Very pleased!

product_title: Brewista Cold Pro Jr. Gusseted Original Paper Filter Filter-25 Pack (BECPJRF25), 2 Liter System
clean_review_title: seasoned cold brewer here, lots of info below, brewer works great, easy to use
clean_review_text: I have been drinking "cold brew" for well over a decade now. The occasional coffee house would have a cold brew system, and so it was a treat back then to purchase a cup of extra strong and extra smooth coffee. Then I started making it at home. There were no special brew systems yet available. So I would simply take a big pitcher dump a bunch of ground coffee into it, then top it off with water. Cover, let sit for a full day, then either carefully pour into a cup as desired (while trying to keep grounds from falling out), or when I was feeling more motivated I would strain through a cheesecloth. Either way, I had easy cold brewed coffee. YUM. Forward to the past several years where cold brew coffee has hit critical mass. Most everyone has heard of it, most coffee shops offer it, and many stores sell it. Many cold brew systems are available, just in different styles. Some coffee pot systems are trying to state that they offer cold brew option- but be aware-- cold brew is NOT ICED COFFEE. It is only cold/room temp water on ground coffee, there is no heat applied at any point (save for if you decide to heat it up after the fact). Ok, so.. this brewer: I have tried a few different types of cold brew systems since my days of dumping loose ground coffee in a pitcher. These days things are a little more stream lined and a tad easier. None of them do anything extra special, it is really just about your preference. It basically breaks down to needing a filter of some sort (can be resuable metal cylinder or paper, like this one has), a vessel to "brew" in. That's it. This system happens to have a little extra in its design. I thought it might be a little much, but I found it pleasant to use, and there was no mess. There appears to be many parts, but it is super easy to figure out. Instructions are super easy to understand if need be. Steps: --The brew container* (see additional info on this part below) is what what you will use to "brew" your coffee. The ratio is 1:8? 8 ounces (1/2 lb) of coffee to about 64 ounces of cold or room temp water. This does not need to be exact. You will not mess up a batch of cold brew if you use a little too much or a little less (though more would be better since you can water it down later if need be). You put course ground coffee into the included paper filter. If not already in base, then place coffee filled bag in side. Put cover on, and set aside on counter or in fridge for 12-24 hours. I usually wait a full day. Sometimes I go longer depending on when I started it. In this case, it was late afternoon, and I kept it on counter for a full day and a half so I could drink some in the morning. *1. the brew vessel has a lever and stopper you will need to attach. Super quick and easy. Make sure the level is in the stop/closed position before adding water. *2. Also, there is a 2 pack of carafe filters (small round sponges) that are optional use. I find they are easy to rinse and reuse. --Once you are done "brewing", put the brewing vessel on top of the grooved pitcher. This is where the lever on the brewing vessel comes in. Push it open. This immediately drains your cold brew into a pourable pitcher. Once the brew vessel is empty, take out bag full of spent coffee and toss it out (OR if you are super thrifty and careful.. perhaps rinse and reuse?). Your pitcher is now full of delicious, smooth, concentrated coffee. The pitcher comes with a lid. The pitcher is of a workable size- easy to hold, small enough to store in fridge, yet holds nearly 2 liters. The size is perfect for storing in the fridge. Indents in the pitcher make it easy to grab/hold. EDIT: Unlike other brew systems, it dawned on me that I could get another batch "brewing" at any time after the first one. This is because the brew container is separate from the storage container. My other "brewer" is a brewer and pitcher in one. So not until I finish the container (or find a different container to store concentrate in) can I make another batch. With this system, I can simply start another even if storage pitcher is in use (so long as I can access it it after brewing, which I tend to brew for 24-36 hours). Anyway, thought I would come back to add that in since it wasn't an immediate thought when I was using this initially. "Cold brew" is considered a coffee concentrate. The idea is to have a strong coffee that can withstand being watered down by ice and other coffee additions (cream, syrups, etc) without losing the coffee taste. Or you can drink it straight if you like very strong coffee (I do!). In addition to that, it makes a very smooth coffee. Usually reduces/removes acidity. And lastly, although it seems like it is only consumed cold, it technically can be warmed up to be enjoyed hot. Some people cold brew to reduce the acidity, and then want to still have warm/hot coffee. Go for it. For me, I just really like the added oomph of taste that cold brew coffee makes. I have even noticed that it can make kinda blah coffee even better by being cold brewed. (have you ever purchased a container of coffee and not really liked it? Try cold brewing it-- it just might change things enough to make it palatable!). Specially about this system: In short, I like it. I first thought it was going to be too many parts to deal with, but I see that the parts it comes with that once they are on, no need to take them off (save for if your brewer needs extra TLC when cleaning). The brew vessel AND a pitcher is more than what is really needed (often I see that a vessel could be the pitcher just by taking bag/filter out)- but this makes it super clean and easy to deal with. I DO like the paper filter over a metal filter in terms of speed of clean up, but some people would prefer not to have excess waste. This style of system is really going to come down to preference. All the parts on mine look good, work soundly, and I have no problem with using this. With one caveat, the pitcher fits into the brew vessel for easy, compact storage (put lid on pitcher, but find spot for vessel lid). This system comes with TWO PLA bag filters, so you will need more. I do not know what this system costs but it doesn't need to be expensive to be effective. I see that the 25 pack of the filters is $15 (or less if buying 25 pack of regular filters). I have a glass with metal filter system that works well for around $25 although makes half as much at a time). Or just use my old school method of a pitcher with loose coffee in it. :) If you choose this, know that it absolutely makes a great pitcher of cold brew coffee concentrate. No problems there. Because I do not know what the price is, I can't automatically recommend this- but I would nudge people in the direction of this style. I personally think it is even easier than the metal filter style, and this makes a decent amount- not too much, but not too little. If it is within reason of other brewers, I would be real pleased. While I normally don't take into consideration price, I do if there are enough of similar products on market-- and knowing that cold brew systems are pretty much all the same except for filter material- that I can comfortably comment on it. Therefor I would normally ask, why would I recommend this over other brewers? That is when price might come into play, along with some other design features. Since this also requires use of paper filter, that is an additional cost. This just comes with two filters, so you would pretty much need to add in the additional cost right away. Say, if I was ordering this I would also order a pack of filters at same time. What would improve upon product: While the pitcher and lid fits into the brewing vessel for more compact storage, the lid of the brewing vessel has nowhere to go. It could be placed on bottom so all of it just sits on it, or it can be placed on top but there again, it is just resting on it. It doesn't make for perfectly tidy storage. It would work if the brew vessel was taller or the pitcher was shorter-- then the pitcher would nestle down into the brew vessel and the lid could just go on top. The difference doesn't seem like it would be enough to displace water/coffee ratio, but would certainly add to the tidiness of storing it. Current rating: 5 stars. It totally works as intended, and I have used enough cold brewers of various styles to be extra pleased with this one. I really didn't think it would be better than my simple glass pitcher with metal filter, but the paper filter and ease of separating the brewed coffee into a separate pitcher has impressed me. This system may be my new go-to brewer and I will pass my other one on to a friend. This is a matter of preference for me. Ultimately the rating goes toward the fact that it works well and produces a solid "cold brew" end result. I have been drinking a cup (ok, maybe 3) while writing this so excuse my length. I feel like writing a dissertation on cold brew while simultaneously running full speed on my treadmill. WEEEEEEEEEEe cold brew is fun! :)

product_title: Fantasy Fairy Coloring Book in Grayscale - Spiral Bound Special Artist Edition Fantasy Fairy Adult Coloring Book on Cardstock by Molly Harrison
clean_review_title: Finally, a book printed on gorgeous paper!
clean_review_text: I received both of Molly's newest books yesterday. They are absolutely gorgeous!!! I wish I could thank Molly personally. Since I can't, this will have to do. The books take a little bit longer to come, I presume because Molly has become the manufacturer. The wait is definitely worth it. The books are now spiral bound at the top of the page. Fantastic for those of us who are lefties!!! Wahoo!!! Thank you Molly! Now here's the very best part, aside from Molly's gorgeous artwork, the paper. I have said for some time now, along with many other Colorists, that I would pay more for a book with better paper. Well here's your opportunity! Molly is printing her books now, on what I believe is a beautiful multi-purpose paper. I rarely use markers, but I think it will take them beautifully. The tooth of the paper is heavenly. Pencils scream for joy (LOL) when used on this paper. If you wanted to color a masterpiece, here is your opportunity. If you enjoy pencils, you are very going to love Molly's two new books. The books are different from each other. This one is comprised of her favorite grayscale pictures, and the other her favorite line art pictures. So the art in these books has been pretty printed in her previous books. If you have never bought any of Molly's books before, I suggest that you start with either this one, or the other other one; which is compromised of her favorite line art. Yes, these books are a little bit more expensive, but they are what most of us have been asking for, for several years. I can hardly wait to put pencil to paper, but I have another WIP to finish first. If you are one of many who have been asking the artists to print their books on something other than Create Space paper, today is the day! Have a blessed day and color on!

clean_review_title: Beet Root Powder For A Good Price
clean_review_text: Beet root powder may have some health benefits, mainly due to the high nitrate content of beets. This gets converted into nitric oxide in the body, which dilates blood vessels, increasing blood flow. This may not only lower blood pressure, but also enhance exercise and athletic related activities. Beet root is also rich in betaine, also known as trimethylglycine (TMG). It has been shown to have a variety of health benefits, including possibly helping with weight loss and muscle gain. I have taken it for years. This powder seems highly concentrated and doesn't taste too bad; it has a kind of sweet and earthy taste, but I wouldn't call it amazing. It is worth trying before exercise, or just supplemented with a meal (I have put it in vegetable smoothies). I haven't noticed any particular benefits from it, like increased endurance, but I also lift regularly and use other forms of pre-workout, so it might be hard for me to see a benefit. So, if you're looking for beet root powder for a good price, check this out.

I think it makes for a quick snack- either to take care of a sweet tooth need, or just need something to nosh on to take care of hungries before next meal. When I am on the go all day, I often dont eat anything until dinner (Im just not hungry, and copious amounts of coffee and/or a smoothie takes care of my need to masticate a meal), but.. sometimes I do get a little hungry. OK, sometimes a lot. lol. I pack some snack bars just in case, but sometimes I want a better mouth feel/different texture than what a dry bar provides. Now I can have one of these and maybe a handful of nuts, and I am satisfied enough to hold off until dinner. Has a light, mild taste. I have another yam product I prefer (about as mild, but little more chew), but I would totally buy these again. If only because I can control myself better. This is 80 calories per stick, where as the other is a bag at 180 per serving/bag. Not bad, but I dont always need that much at once (but I cant control myself haha). So different items purchased for different reasons. product_title: Biz Laundry Detergent Powder Booster, Stain & Odor Removal - 80 Ounces
clean_review_text: I was told by my boss at a dry cleaners that this is the domestic version of the commercial stain remover he uses in his process. You cannot believe the results he gets! After running out of the bag he gave me, I tried this formulation. It takes considerably more, but is worth it. It removes stains of all types and whitens beautifully. Best results are achieved by using hot water and giving a soak. But it will also work in lower temps. Hint: Run an inch or two of water in your bathtub. Add some BIZ and detergent. Soak. Squeeze, don't wring, don't drain, and then wash in a normal wash. I do heavy stains overnight, then lesser ones in the same water. This removed blue shampoo from a white cotton shower curtain that was marked cold, hang to dry! That saved me the cost of replacing the curtain.

clean_review_text: Co-enzyme Q10 has been shown to have multiple benefits, and I have taken it for many years as a general anti-aging supplement. This supplement is labeled ubidecarenone, which is basically a synonym for ubiquinone, the more common name for co-enzyme Q10. I admit I have never seen that chemical name used, so I had to look it up. This is co-enzyme Q10 that is supposed to be absorbed better. I'm guessing this is because it is mixed with lecithin, which has been shown to enhance the absorption of co-enzyme Q10 and a variety of other chemicals. Since co-enzyme Q10 can often be challenging to absorb, this seems like a good formula.

clean_review_title: Provide cushioning but will make your shoes feel tighter
clean_review_text: PerseveranX Shoe Inserts The shoe inserts arrived in a plain pink box and included no instructions. Not that instructions are specifically needed, but I found it kind of odd there was nothing but the box and the pads. There are enough for two pairs of shoes. Upon seeing the thickness, I was afraid they would make my shoes too tight. I was pleasantly surprised when I put on my shoes and they felt comfortable; a little tighter, but still comfortable. The information provided on the product page does warn that the inserts will take up some space in your shoes and recommends buying up a shoe size. This isn’t going to help if you already have the shoes. The inserts do exactly what they claim to do in that they relieve some of the discomfort to my feet when I wear high heels. I didn’t notice that my feet were any hotter or sweatier than they would have been without the inserts (it was a very warm day). My only complaint is that with open-sided shoes, the blue foam shows. I’m not sure how long the cushioning effect will last until the pads have to be replaced, but so far, so good.
